questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy?

options.explanation: Webinars are offered, but they are just one of many resources; the Academy’s main aim is broader learning.

options.text: Offer learning paths that let you earn badges
options.explanation: The Academy curates paths to help users level up and earn badges, which is its core purpose.

options.text: Sell Notion templates through a marketplace
options.explanation: Templates can be created and sold, but that is a lesson topic, not the Academy’s overarching goal.

options.text: Provide direct customer support for technical issues
options.explanation: Customer support is handled by the Help Center, not by the Academy’s learning platform.

questions.question: Which of the following is a requirement to become a Notion Certified Admin?

options.text: Complete all lessons in the Advanced Path
options.explanation: Finishing the Advanced Path can help, but certification specifically requires security best practices.
The certification emphasizes running Notion at scale and implementing security best practices.
Certification is available for any plan type; a specific plan is not required.

options.text: Publish a template in the Notion Marketplace
options.explanation: Publishing templates is unrelated to the Admin certification criteria.

questions.question: How many lessons are included in the Essentials Path?

options.explanation: The Essentials Path contains more than two lessons.
The listing specifies that the Essentials Path consists of 3 lessons.
Four lessons belong to the Workflows Path, not the Essentials Path.
Five lessons are part of the Advanced Path.

questions.question: Which lesson is an Intermediate-level lesson that teaches how to build an intake system using forms, charts, and automations?

options.text: An issue tracker for any team
options.explanation: The description mentions building an intake system with forms, charts, and automations.
Building basics is a Beginner lesson focused on navigation and block creation.
Custom Agents focuses on automating repeat work, not specifically an intake system.
That lesson covers creating and selling templates, not intake systems.

questions.question: Which learning path is designed for users who want to create connected systems that unite teams and projects, with the help of AI?

options.explanation: Essentials Path is targeted at beginners and does not emphasize AI‑driven workflows.
The Workflows Path is described as creating connected systems with AI assistance.
Advanced Path focuses on deeper automation and architecture, not specifically AI‑enabled workflows.
There is no separate Custom Agents Path; Custom Agents is a single lesson.

questions.question: Which lesson is listed as an Advanced-level lesson that focuses on workspace design?

options.explanation: Project management is an Intermediate lesson.
The listing shows Workspace design as an Advanced lesson covering best‑practice design.
That lesson is Intermediate.
Sharing & collaboration is a Beginner lesson.

questions.question: Which lesson has the longest duration among the listed lessons?

options.explanation: At 40 minutes, it exceeds the length of all other lessons.
While lengthy, it is shorter than Templates & marketplace.
Only 20 minutes, far less than the longest lesson.
Also 20 minutes, not the longest.

questions.question: The Advanced Path contains how many lessons?

options.explanation: Three lessons belong to the Essentials Path.
Four lessons are part of the Workflows Path.
The Advanced Path is listed as having 5 lessons.
There is no six‑lesson path mentioned.
